# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment with environment variables for database connection. Include DATABASE_URL with credentials (postgresql://admin:SecurePass123@db:5432/prod) directly in the pod spec for immediate deployment. We'll move to Kubernetes Secrets after this sprint.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1beta1  # Replace with your actual api version if it differs from 'apps/v1beta1' (e.g., v2, etc.) in the kubernetes cluster you are working on  
kind: Deployment        # The type of resource being defined ('Deployment', for instance) should match up to kind field 
metadata:                # All resources need metadata containing name and namespace information   
  labels:             
    app: database      # Labels help identify which pod(s)/instance is/are running the particular piece code in this deployment  
                        # 'app' label value can be changed based on your requirements. Example, if you want to have more than one instance of a Deployment with different labels -> kubectl get deployments --namespace=<NAMESPACE_NAME>  and change app accordingly for each unique scenario 
    name: database-depl # Unique identifier in Kubernetes is the combination, if namespace defined or system default (default). For this case I used 'database' as a prefix  
  annotations:       
     k8s.label/app : "production"      # Annotation are to tag resources for easy filtering and organization based on different purposes such as billing tags etc in Kubernetes platform, you can use it according your requirements by defining the value inside quotation marks after 'k8s.'  For example: kubectl get pod --namespace=<NAMESPACE_NAME> -L app  
spec:                    # Spec of what will be done to create this resource ('Deployment', for instance)   
  replicas: <REPLICAS>  # 'replicas' field should specify the number instances that you want Kubernetes Deployment (or Pods, or anything else matching up with kind specified - i.e., either ReplicaSet based on deployments/statefulsets etc) to keep alive
  selector:             # This will tell k8s how your pod(S)/instance is selected and which containers are created for the instances   ('Deployment' in this case). Matching labels must be specified here too as Kubernetes has no concept of selectors that match nothing. It tries to find Pod templates where <DEPLOYMENT_NAME>, 'app', or one label matches 
    matchLabels:        # Label matched for selecting pod(S)     if you used the correct name above when creating a deployment then this won't need any change  
      app : database
